title: Terminal int8 hard-sigmoid block
hypothesis: A bounded int8 hard-sigmoid activation block can be implemented and physically characterized well enough to serve as another low-risk nonlinear circuit_block reference using the existing integer pwl path.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can one bounded int8 hard-sigmoid block produce accepted physical implementation metrics suitable for later integrated and Layer 2 evaluation use?
include: one bounded int8 hard-sigmoid block candidate
one bounded physical sweep on the target platform
exclude: integrated nm1 architecture-block evaluation in the first pass
native fp16 hard-sigmoid in the first pass
follow_on_broad_sweep: only if the first hard-sigmoid block is accepted and broader nonlinear-family exploration becomes necessary

expected_benefit: provides real physical timing, power, and area for another bounded int8 nonlinear activation block
gives a small real evaluation task to exercise automatic merge-to-finalization without broader architecture risk
risks: the first point set may need adjustment for clamp behavior around the piecewise breakpoints
later integrated use may still require a separate architecture-block follow-on

expected_result.reason: the first goal is simply to obtain accepted physical implementation metrics for one bounded int8 hard-sigmoid block
